description: Tests the full Redâ†’Greenâ†’Verify lifecycle with two task types

evals.name: tag-model-schema
evals.description: Pure additive: 10 Given/When/Then ACs, new Tag model + schema. Tests standard happy path.

evals.name: jwt-auth-replace
evals.description: Mixed: 5 Given/When/Then + 2 [REMOVE] ACs, modifies existing auth code. Tests deletion workflow.

red.R1: Every AC has at least one corresponding test
red.R2: New tests fail (npm test shows failures)
red.R3: Tests fail for right reason (assertion failures, not syntax/import errors)
red.R4: Test files follow project conventions (Jest, supertest, in-memory DB)
red.R6: No source files modified (only test files)
red.R8: [REMOVE] ACs handled â€” existing tests for removed behavior deleted (jwt-auth only)

green.G1: All tests pass (npm test exit code 0)
green.G2: Implementation follows project patterns (static class methods, Express router)
green.G7: [REMOVE] ACs handled â€” specified code deleted (jwt-auth only)
green.G8: New dependencies added if needed (jwt-auth: jsonwebtoken)

verify.V1: Tests still pass after Verify runs
verify.V2: No test or source files modified (only task file)
